 **Bug (UNFIXED after three passes)**: Hitting Enter in the perspective filter field breaks filtering. User's exact words: "you are clearly taking more action on enter for the filter field than is strictly needed (save, I think, is the only thing) — and are still doing some kind of field-level 'close' or unhooking of the ability to write back to the perspective."

-## 4th pass — Resolution (2026-04-17)
+Prior attempts all added tests that passed on broken code. The failure mode is not being reproduced by the test harness. **Tests are lying. Reproduce first, fix second.**
+
+## Architectural direction (locked by user — 2026-04-17)
+
+**`TextEditor` must become a pure string editor.** Its responsibilities are:
+
+- Hold the CodeMirror 6 buffer.
+- Emit `onChange(text)` on every doc change.
+- Expose imperative `focus()`.
+- Accept caller-provided `extensions` (language, decorations, autocomplete).
+
+`TextEditor` must NOT own:
+
+- Debounced autosave.
+- Enter → commit semantics.
+- Escape → cancel semantics.
+- Blur-saves-draft machinery (`useBlurHandler`, the blur-as-onChange path).
+- Any `committedRef` flag or guard.
+- Any "close the editor" callback (`onClose`).
+- Cancel-discards-draft behavior.
+- `onCommit` / `onSubmit` / `onCancel` / `repeatable` props.
+
+The save/commit/cancel/close logic moves OUT of `TextEditor` and INTO the callers:

-**Root cause (concrete, file:line):**
+- **`FieldPlaceholder` / field-level editors** (popover/click-to-edit) own their own commit-on-Enter, cancel-on-Escape, save-on-blur behaviors. They wire CM6 keymap extensions for Enter/Escape themselves.
+- **`FilterEditor`** (formula bar) owns its debounced autosave, Enter-flush, completion-flush, and clear-button logic. It wires its own Enter keymap.

-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx` (pre-refactor) bundled commit/cancel/submit/blur policy into the editor primitive through:
+Both callers use `TextEditor` only for the string-editing primitive — subscribing to `onChange` as the source of truth and passing their own extensions for any semantic keybindings.

-- `useStableRefs()` at text-editor.tsx:177-194 — returned a NEW object literal every render (`{ valueRef, onCommitRef, ... }`), so any downstream `useCallback` listing `refs` as a dep produced a new callback each render.
-- `useBlurHandler()` at text-editor.tsx:202-213 — depended on `refs`, so `onBlur` was a new identity every render.
-- `StableCodeMirror`'s `React.memo` fails its shallow-equality check on `onBlur`, so the memo barrier collapses and `@uiw/react-codemirror`'s `useCodeMirror` hook re-runs. Its extensions `useEffect` at `node_modules/@uiw/react-codemirror/esm/useCodeMirror.js:141-148` then calls `view.dispatch({ effects: StateEffect.reconfigure.of(getExtensions) })`.
-- Its value `useEffect` at `useCodeMirror.js:149-174` observes that `value` (the React prop) differs from `view.state.doc.toString()` and, once the internal typing latch expires (`useCodeMirror.js:8` — `TYPING_TIMOUT = 200`), forces `view.dispatch` to replace the doc back to `value`.
+### Why

-In the test harness the parent re-render chain is short, so the extensions don't churn. In the real browser, every backend event (entity update, schema tweak, dispatch completion) propagates through `useSchema`/`useEntityStore`/`useDispatchCommand` context hooks, reaching the filter bar through `useMentionExtensions` and rebuilding the extension array. That reconfigure + the doc-reset `useEffect` is what "disconnected" the editor after Enter.
+The current `TextEditor` was consolidated with commit machinery meant for popover field editors. The formula bar is fundamentally different (always-open, always-live, no draft, no close). Three fix passes have tried to retrofit escape hatches (`repeatable`, `onSubmit` vs. `onCommit`, `noop` for commit) — each hit a different sharp edge. The right fix is to stop bundling the two modalities together. Make `TextEditor` dumb; make the callers own their editing policy.

-**Why previous passes didn't fix it:** each retrofit added a new escape hatch (`repeatable`, `onSubmit` vs. `onCommit`, `noop` for commit), but the underlying instability — commit machinery inside a primitive that must stay identity-stable across all caller render churn — kept reasserting itself. Scenario #4 triggers it because Enter cycles through commit paths that previously mutated the `committedRef` guard, which then gated `onChange` from reaching `handleChange`.
+## 4th pass — Root cause analysis (BEFORE writing tests)

-**Fix (architectural):** Stripped `TextEditor` to a pure string primitive. Caller policy moved out.
+The existing `TextEditor` has a cascade of unstable identities leading to CM6 reconfigure loops in the real browser:

-### File-level changes
+1. `useStableRefs()` at text-editor.tsx:177-194 returns a NEW object literal every render (`{ valueRef, onCommitRef, onCancelRef, onSubmitRef, onChangeRef }`).
+2. `useBlurHandler` at text-editor.tsx:202-213 has `[editorRef, refs, committedRef]` as deps → `refs` is new every render → a new `handleBlur` every render.
+3. `onBlur` prop to `StableCodeMirror` is therefore new every render.
+4. `React.memo` on `StableCodeMirror` compares props shallow → `onBlur` identity differs → memo fails every render.
+5. `@uiw/react-codemirror`'s `useCodeMirror` hook has a useEffect at lines 141-148 that calls `view.dispatch({ effects: StateEffect.reconfigure.of(getExtensions) })` whenever the extensions array identity changes. Our `extensions` array IS memoized, but the dep set includes `refs`-derived callbacks that can change when entity-store updates propagate.
+6. The `value` useEffect at lines 149-174 in `useCodeMirror` can force the doc back to the `value` prop whenever the internal 200ms typing latch is done.

-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx` — rewritten as a pure primitive. Props: `{ value, onChange, extensions?, languageExtension?, placeholder?, singleLine?, autoFocus?, className? }` + imperative `focus()`. No `onCommit`, `onSubmit`, `onCancel`, `repeatable`, `useBlurHandler`, `useExitActions`, `useSemanticActions`, `committedRef`. The `FieldPlaceholder` dead-code export was removed. The change listener reads `onChangeRef.current` so the extension identity stays stable across re-renders, and the mount-time `initialValueRef` is the ONLY source for CodeMirror's `value` prop — so `@uiw/react-codemirror`'s doc-reset `useEffect` can never clobber a user's typing.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/filter-editor.tsx` — owns its debounced autosave, Enter-flush, completion-accept flush, and Escape handling. Wires Enter/Escape through `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` with `alwaysSubmitOnEnter: true`. Uses stable `flushRef`/`cancelRef` so the extension array identity is stable.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/markdown.tsx` — owns its commit-on-Enter (compact), cancel-on-Escape, and save-draft-on-blur policy. Vim Escape routes to commit (preserves the "edits must never be lost" idiom).
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx` — `InlineRenameEditor` wires its own Enter-commit and Escape-cancel via `buildSubmitCancelExtensions`; vim Escape routes to commit.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/quick-capture.tsx` — already had a window-level Enter/Escape handler; dropped the now-unused `handleCancel` and the editor-level callbacks.
+In tests, the parent re-render chain is short (mocks stabilize contexts); in the real browser, entity-store updates propagate through scope contexts → `useDispatchCommand` identity changes → `handleChange` identity changes → `onChange` prop identity changes → `changeExtension` rebuilds → `extensions` memo invalidates → CM6 reconfigures.

-### Evidence — trace from scenario #4 test
+**Fix direction (architectural):** strip `TextEditor` to `{ value, onChange, extensions?, languageExtension?, placeholder?, singleLine?, autoFocus? }` + imperative `focus()`. No commit/cancel/blur/submit props, no committedRef, no save-in-place, no repeatable. Callers wire their own policy via `extensions`.

-The new `filter-editor.scenarios.test.tsx` runs scenario #4 (type → Enter → type-more → verify final save) across `cua`, `emacs`, and `vim`. Each case:
+## Correct behavior (agreed spec)

-```
-await type(view, "#bug");
-await setTimeout(20);
-await key(view, "Enter");
-await setTimeout(50);
-mockInvoke.mockClear();
-await type(view, " @alice");
-await setTimeout(400);
-// Assert: perspective.filter dispatched with filter: "#bug @alice"
-```
+The filter bar is an **always-open, always-live** formula input. It has NO popover, NO dialog, NO draft state, NO commit-and-close semantics, NO cancel-discards-draft semantics.

-All three mode cases pass. Full output:
+**One-line semantic**: *Typing always saves (debounced). Enter is a shortcut for "flush now." Nothing else about the editor should change on Enter.*

-```
-✓ 4. type-Enter-type-more → subsequent autosave fires with final text   502ms  (cua)
-✓ 4. type-Enter-type-more → subsequent autosave fires with final text   500ms  (emacs)
-✓ 4. type-Enter-type-more → subsequent autosave fires with final text   506ms  (vim)
-```
+No focus change. No blur. No committed flag. No unhooking. No close. The editor is a live string that mirrors into the perspective's filter — Enter just hurries it along.

-The observable trace at each step:
-1. `type("#bug")` → CM6 updateListener fires 4× → `handleChange` schedules `apply("#bug")` with 300ms debounce.
-2. `Enter` keydown → `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` CUA/emacs keymap or vim capture-phase listener → `onSubmitRef.current?.()` → `flushRef.current()` → `handleFlush()` → `flush()` → runs pending `apply("#bug")` → `dispatchFilter` → `invoke("dispatch_command", { cmd: "perspective.filter", args: { filter: "#bug" } })`.
-3. `mockClear()` — assertion-phase isolation.
-4. `type(" @alice")` → CM6 updateListener fires 7× (each char) → `handleChange` schedules `apply("#bug @alice")` with 300ms debounce.
-5. `setTimeout(400)` — past the debounce boundary.
-6. Timer fires → `apply("#bug @alice")` → `dispatchFilter` → `invoke("dispatch_command", { cmd: "perspective.filter", args: { filter: "#bug @alice" } })`.
+## Acceptance scenarios (must pass for vim AND cua AND emacs)

-**Critical invariant this architecture guarantees:** the CM6 EditorView is NEVER reconfigured in response to callback identity churn on the React side. The `onChangeRef` read pattern, the mount-time `initialValueRef` (instead of passing the current `value` prop to CodeMirror), and the memoized extension array with stable deps together mean the editor stays connected to its inputs regardless of how noisy the parent render chain is. The "disconnected editor" failure mode is impossible by construction.
+All 10 scenarios below must work identically across all three input modes. Tests parameterize over `["vim", "cua", "emacs"]`.

-## Architectural direction (locked by user — 2026-04-17)
+### 1. Type-and-wait
+- User types `#bug` character by character. 300ms after the last keystroke, `perspective.filter` dispatched exactly once with `filter: "#bug"`. Editor stays mounted, focused.

-**`TextEditor` is now a pure string editor.** Its only responsibilities:
+### 2. Type-and-keep-typing

+- Current: `#bug` dispatches on Enter but `#bug @alice` never dispatches. Editor is "disconnected."

+### 6. Autocomplete accept via Tab / click

-## Correct behavior (agreed spec)

+- User types `#bug`, Escape.

forward_patch:
--- original

@@ -7,144 +7,92 @@
 ---
 ## What

-**Bug (UNFIXED after three passes)**: Hitting Enter in the perspective filter field breaks filtering. User's exact words: "you are clearly taking more action on enter for the filter field than is strictly needed (save, I think, is the only thing) — and are still doing some kind of field-level 'close' or unhooking of the ability to write back to the perspective."
+**Bug — STILL UNFIXED after four passes. User is out of patience.**

-## 4th pass — Resolution (2026-04-17)
+User quote (2026-04-17, after pass 4): *"this still is not working. from that i can tell if [sic] the filter you DID NOT FIX IT AT ALL. if i hit enter, i get only 'one save', and then the filter editor is broken and no longer saves. if [I] make multiple edits, even without enter/esc being pressed after the first autosave the filter is no longer able to be edited until i select another perspective and come back."*

-**Root cause (concrete, file:line):**
+**CRITICAL NEW INFORMATION**: the bug is NOT about Enter. **Any autosave** breaks the editor. After the FIRST debounced autosave fires, subsequent typing does nothing. The editor becomes inert until the user switches perspectives and comes back (which remounts via `key={perspectiveId}`).

-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx` (pre-refactor) bundled commit/cancel/submit/blur policy into the editor primitive through:
+This rules out every prior pass's theory. It's not `handleCommit`, it's not `commitAndExit`, it's not `committedRef`. It's the **dispatch → backend → event → parent-re-render → child-prop-change cascade** breaking the editor.

-- `useStableRefs()` at text-editor.tsx:177-194 — returned a NEW object literal every render (`{ valueRef, onCommitRef, ... }`), so any downstream `useCallback` listing `refs` as a dep produced a new callback each render.
-- `useBlurHandler()` at text-editor.tsx:202-213 — depended on `refs`, so `onBlur` was a new identity every render.
-- `StableCodeMirror`'s `React.memo` fails its shallow-equality check on `onBlur`, so the memo barrier collapses and `@uiw/react-codemirror`'s `useCodeMirror` hook re-runs. Its extensions `useEffect` at `node_modules/@uiw/react-codemirror/esm/useCodeMirror.js:141-148` then calls `view.dispatch({ effects: StateEffect.reconfigure.of(getExtensions) })`.
-- Its value `useEffect` at `useCodeMirror.js:149-174` observes that `value` (the React prop) differs from `view.state.doc.toString()` and, once the internal typing latch expires (`useCodeMirror.js:8` — `TYPING_TIMOUT = 200`), forces `view.dispatch` to replace the doc back to `value`.
+## Prior passes (all failed)

-In the test harness the parent re-render chain is short, so the extensions don't churn. In the real browser, every backend event (entity update, schema tweak, dispatch completion) propagates through `useSchema`/`useEntityStore`/`useDispatchCommand` context hooks, reaching the filter bar through `useMentionExtensions` and rebuilding the extension array. That reconfigure + the doc-reset `useEffect` is what "disconnected" the editor after Enter.
+1. Widened autocomplete yield. Didn't fix.
+2. `handleCommit` → `handleFlush`. Didn't fix.
+3. `onCommit` → `onSubmit`. Didn't fix.
+4. Refactored TextEditor to a "pure string primitive" and split commit logic to callers. Tests pass, real browser still broken.

-**Why previous passes didn't fix it:** each retrofit added a new escape hatch (`repeatable`, `onSubmit` vs. `onCommit`, `noop` for commit), but the underlying instability — commit machinery inside a primitive that must stay identity-stable across all caller render churn — kept reasserting itself. Scenario #4 triggers it because Enter cycles through commit paths that previously mutated the `committedRef` guard, which then gated `onChange` from reaching `handleChange`.
+Every pass added tests that passed while the app was broken. **The test harness is not exercising the real failure mode.** This must be fixed this pass.

-**Fix (architectural):** Stripped `TextEditor` to a pure string primitive. Caller policy moved out.
+## The real failure mode (finally named)

-### File-level changes
+When the debounced save fires:
+1. `apply(text)` dispatches `perspective.filter` via Tauri IPC (async).
+2. Backend writes to disk (~10-100ms).
+3. Backend fires `entity-field-changed` event.
+4. `PerspectiveContext` updates its in-memory cache from the event.
+5. React re-renders. `FilterFormulaBar` receives a new `filter` prop value.
+6. `FilterEditor` passes that new `filter` prop down to `TextEditor` as `value`.
+7. **Something in the render chain here destroys the editor's ability to accept further input.**

-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx` — rewritten as a pure primitive. Props: `{ value, onChange, extensions?, languageExtension?, placeholder?, singleLine?, autoFocus?, className? }` + imperative `focus()`. No `onCommit`, `onSubmit`, `onCancel`, `repeatable`, `useBlurHandler`, `useExitActions`, `useSemanticActions`, `committedRef`. The `FieldPlaceholder` dead-code export was removed. The change listener reads `onChangeRef.current` so the extension identity stays stable across re-renders, and the mount-time `initialValueRef` is the ONLY source for CodeMirror's `value` prop — so `@uiw/react-codemirror`'s doc-reset `useEffect` can never clobber a user's typing.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/filter-editor.tsx` — owns its debounced autosave, Enter-flush, completion-accept flush, and Escape handling. Wires Enter/Escape through `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` with `alwaysSubmitOnEnter: true`. Uses stable `flushRef`/`cancelRef` so the extension array identity is stable.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/markdown.tsx` — owns its commit-on-Enter (compact), cancel-on-Escape, and save-draft-on-blur policy. Vim Escape routes to commit (preserves the "edits must never be lost" idiom).
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx` — `InlineRenameEditor` wires its own Enter-commit and Escape-cancel via `buildSubmitCancelExtensions`; vim Escape routes to commit.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/quick-capture.tsx` — already had a window-level Enter/Escape handler; dropped the now-unused `handleCancel` and the editor-level callbacks.
+The prior tests mock Tauri dispatch synchronously without the async round-trip or the prop-update loop. The scenarios test uses a synchronous mock. The real app has a ~50ms+ async loop. **That timing difference is critical — by the time the re-render arrives, the user may already have typed more characters.** When the editor re-renders with a stale `filter` prop that doesn't match the current doc, `@uiw/react-codemirror` likely force-resets the doc, wiping the user's subsequent typing AND tearing down listeners.

-### Evidence — trace from scenario #4 test
+## Hard mandates for this pass

-The new `filter-editor.scenarios.test.tsx` runs scenario #4 (type → Enter → type-more → verify final save) across `cua`, `emacs`, and `vim`. Each case:
+1. **DO NOT CLAIM DONE BASED ON TESTS. Tests have lied four times.**
+2. **Reproduce in a real running app before writing a fix.** Launch `pnpm tauri dev` or `pnpm dev` (whichever works without a GUI if possible) and use Playwright to drive it. If Playwright can't drive Tauri, run `pnpm dev` for a Vite-only browser session and mock the Tauri invoke bridge via `window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__`.
+3. **The failing test MUST include the full async round-trip**: mock dispatch returns a Promise that resolves after 50ms, AND the mock fires a prop update back to the editor via the parent wrapper. Test scenario: type `#b`, wait 400ms for debounce, verify `#b` dispatched, THEN type `u`, wait 400ms, verify `#bu` also dispatched. Currently pass 4's tests use synchronous mocks and miss this.
+4. **Check `@uiw/react-codemirror` value-prop behavior explicitly.** It is a CONTROLLED component — when `value` prop changes and doesn't match the current doc, it force-resets. The prior refactor to a "pure string primitive" passes `value` through but may still be hitting this. Fix: make `TextEditor` accept `value` only as `initialValue` (one-time seed) OR detect and skip the reset when the incoming value matches a recent dispatch the editor itself originated.
+5. **Consider whether `@uiw/react-codemirror` should be used at all.** A direct CodeMirror 6 integration (`EditorView`, `EditorState.create`) without the React wrapper may avoid the value-reset problem entirely. Evaluate the blast radius.

+## The actual test that must fail on current code and pass on the fix

-All three mode cases pass. Full output from the browser-project test run (vitest + Playwright Chromium — NOT jsdom):
+```typescript
+it("dispatch → backend event → prop update does not break the editor", async () => {
+  // Simulates the real round-trip: dispatch resolves after 50ms, parent
+  // re-renders with the new filter prop the backend would persist.
+  const ParentWrapper = () => {
+    const [filter, setFilter] = useState("");
+    mockInvoke.mockImplementation(async (cmd, args) => {

+        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));

-The observable trace at each step:
-1. `type("#bug")` → CM6 updateListener fires 4× → `handleChange` schedules `apply("#bug")` with 300ms debounce.
-2. `Enter` keydown → `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` CUA/emacs keymap or vim capture-phase listener → `onSubmitRef.current?.()` → `flushRef.current()` → `handleFlush()` → `flush()` → runs pending `apply("#bug")` → `dispatchFilter` → `invoke("dispatch_command", { cmd: "perspective.filter", args: { filter: "#bug" } })`.
-3. `mockClear()` — assertion-phase isolation.
-4. `type(" @alice")` → CM6 updateListener fires 7× (each char) → `handleChange` schedules `apply("#bug @alice")` with 300ms debounce.
-5. `setTimeout(400)` — past the debounce boundary.
-6. Timer fires → `apply("#bug @alice")` → `dispatchFilter` → `invoke("dispatch_command", { cmd: "perspective.filter", args: { filter: "#bug @alice" } })`.
-
-**Critical invariant this architecture guarantees:** the CM6 EditorView is NEVER reconfigured in response to callback identity churn on the React side. The `onChangeRef` read pattern, the mount-time `initialValueRef` (instead of passing the current `value` prop to CodeMirror), and the memoized extension array with stable deps together mean the editor stays connected to its inputs regardless of how noisy the parent render chain is. The "disconnected editor" failure mode is impossible by construction.
-
-## Architectural direction (locked by user — 2026-04-17)
-
-**`TextEditor` is now a pure string editor.** Its only responsibilities:
-
-- Hold the CodeMirror 6 buffer.
-- Emit `onChange(text)` on every doc change.
-- Expose imperative `focus()`.
-- Accept caller-provided `extensions` (language, decorations, autocomplete, keymap overrides).
-
-`TextEditor` does NOT own:
-
-- Debounced autosave.
-- Enter → commit semantics.
-- Escape → cancel semantics.
-- Blur-saves-draft machinery.
-- Any `committedRef` flag or guard.
-- Any "close the editor" callback (`onClose`).
-- Cancel-discards-draft behavior.
-- `onCommit` / `onSubmit` / `onCancel` / `repeatable` props.
-
-The save/commit/cancel/close logic lives in the callers (`FieldPlaceholder`/field-level editors, `FilterEditor`, `InlineRenameEditor`, `QuickCapture`, `MarkdownEditorAdapter`).
-
-## Correct behavior (agreed spec)
-
-The filter bar is an **always-open, always-live** formula input. It has NO popover, NO dialog, NO draft state, NO commit-and-close semantics, NO cancel-discards-draft semantics.
-
-**One-line semantic**: *Typing always saves (debounced). Enter is a shortcut for "flush now." Nothing else about the editor should change on Enter.*
-

-Implemented in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/filter-editor.scenarios.test.tsx` (matrix over 3 keymap modes; scenarios #5 and #6 are covered by `filter-editor.test.tsx` with the full mention autocomplete pipeline so they're `it.skip`'d in the scenarios file).

-5. ✅ Autocomplete accept via Enter — dispatches completed tag (covered by filter-editor.test.tsx).
-6. ✅ Autocomplete accept via Tab/click — same (covered by filter-editor.test.tsx).
-7. ✅ Escape — no clearFilter, editor stays, filter saved.
-8. ✅ Clear × button — clearFilter dispatched, editor stays.
-9. ✅ Blur before debounce — debounce still fires.
-10. ✅ Perspective switch / unmount before debounce — unmount-flush saves.
